title: podman run home directory
body:
/kind feature

**Description**
Automatic assignment of home directory may be incorrect, we need the ability to set the home directory for when a user is specified.


1.
```
podman run --userns=keep-id --rm -i -t --name centos  centos /bin/bash
bash-4.4$ id
uid=1000(mpawlowsky) gid=1000(mpawlowsky) groups=1000(mpawlowsky)
bash-4.4$ echo $HOME
/
bash-4.4$ touch ${HOME}/hello
touch: cannot touch '//hello': Permission denied
bash-4.4$ touch ${HOME}hello
touch: cannot touch '/hello': Permission denied
bash-4.4$ grep mpawlowsky /etc/passwd
mpawlowsky:*:1000:1000::/:/bin/sh
bash-4.4$ 
```


**Describe the results you received:**
Cannot write to the home directory.  Programs such as gradle fail, since they want to create a temporary files in the home directory and ignore the environment variable HOME.  I do not want the root directory to be writable though.

**Describe the results you expected:**
Need ability to specify the home directory on the podman run command that will be used when the entry in /etc/passwd is created.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
```
[mpawlowsky@sigbeehler ~]$ podman exec -u 0:0 -it crazy_driscoll usermod --home /home/mpawlowsky mpawlowsky
usermod: user mpawlowsky is currently used by process 1
[mpawlowsky@sigbeehler ~]$ 
```

Would be nice to have most of the options that exist for useradd, e.g. shell, groups, ...

Using docker exec to change the home directory in /etc/passwd with vi, and creating the home directory, allows gradle to work.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.14.10
Built:        Tue Dec  8 09:37:43 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
$ podman run --userns=keep-id --rm -i -t --name centos  centos grep dwalsh /etc/passwd
dwalsh:*:3267:3267:Daniel J Walsh:/:/bin/sh

The issue is the /home/dwalsh does not exists inside of the container, to it fails.  If I create a homedir with the correct name in the container then it works.

$ podman run --privileged -v /home:/home --userns=keep-id --rm -i -t --name centos  centos grep dwalsh /etc/passwd
dwalsh:*:3267:3267:Daniel J Walsh:/home/dwalsh:/bin/sh

$ podman run --privileged -v pwd:/home/dwalsh --userns=keep-id --rm -i -t --name centos  centos echo $HOME
/home/dwalsh
$ podman run -v ./home:/home/dwalsh:Z --userns=keep-id --rm -i -t --name centos  centos echo $HOME
/home/dwalsh

This looks like it it working perfectly,  I am going to close.

But now we are mapping a directory into the container.  Which is a nice workaround but still not quite right, since the container is leaking into the outside. You also have the assumption that /home is being used vs something like /accounts/d/, ;don't ask legacy stuff.

$ podman run --tmpfs /home/dwash --userns=keep-id --rm -i -t --name centos centos echo $HOME
/home/dwalsh
Or
$ podman run home:/home/dwash --userns=keep-id --rm -i -t --name centos centos echo $HOME
/home/dwalsh

Would also work, which would either create a tmpfs on /home/dwalsh or a volume specific for the container at that site.

Bottom line if an application is going to use $HOME, it is going to expect it to exists.  This is why podman is checking if the homedir exists and then setting it.

Of course you could also run

podman run --env HOME=/tmp ...

But this would not match up with the contents in /etc/passwd.

That worked like a charm.  Thanks you. 
May I recommend that this be mentioned explicitly in the documentation for "podman run".

keep-id: creates a user namespace where the current rootless user’s UID:GID are mapped to the same values in the container. This option is ignored for containers created by the root user.

**Entries will be created in containers /etc/passwd and /etc/group, using the current users home directory .  If the container does not have the home directory then the root (/) will be used.  You may create a temporary home directory using --tmpfs.  Example:  podman run --tmpfs ${HOME} --userns=keep-id --rm  centos echo $HOME**
Please open a PR with this exact change and we can get it merged.
